key_findings: Temporal separation is computationally necessary (mathematical proof, arXiv:2603.04688v1)
Neural criticality degrades during wake, resets during sleep (Nature Neuroscience 2024)
SWS and REM play opposing roles: SWS preserves, REM transforms (Nature Comm Bio 2025)
Hippocampal replay at 10-100x compression (Nature Comm 2026)
Optimal sleep duration ~7h for humans, U-shaped curve
1:2 sleep-wake ratio supports circadian modulation
Consolidation necessity scales with system capacity
Personalized TMR > standard TMR for difficult memories

passes.focus: mathematical modeling, criticality monitoring, and axiomatic derivation
key_findings: Optimal 1:2 ratio (8h:16h) derived from topological weight partition (2,1) from propagation axioms.
Homeostatic Balance Equation for adaptive duty cycles based on encoding load vs. replay compression.
REST (SWS) stabilizes details; WONDER (REM) transforms into abstract gist/DQ.
Computational equivalent of neural avalanches: Activation Avalanche size, EffRank, and Branching Ratio.
Reset derived as recursive resolution of phase lags accumulated due to finite-velocity encoding (Axiom 2).
Predictive Forgetting erases outcome-irrelevant information I(X;Z|Y) to reach Decision Quotient (DQ).

passes.focus: implementation spec for criticality and reality gating
key_findings: Efficient EffRank monitoring via Hutch++ and randomized trace estimators.
Avalanche statistics monitored using Reduced Order Models (ROMs) for < 5% overhead.
Polyphasic 'naps' triggered by EffRank/Avalanche drift are 20-30% more efficient than monophasic schedules.
WONDER mode reality gating using Distance-Aware Temperature Scaling (DATS) and exponential constraint decay.
Koide Q-ratio (2/3) confirmed as a fixed point in Information Bottleneck (IB) flow, driven by Clifford Torus geometry.

passes.focus: empirical validation of criticality metrics and IB phase transitions
key_findings: Hutch++ overhead validated at 120B scale: 1.3-1.8% per iteration (arXiv:2602.00816v1)
DATS lambda recommended: 0.01 (precision), 0.03 (balanced), 0.07 (creative) from analogous systems
IB critical beta* formula: β*_i = 1/(1-λ_i) — phase transitions at eigenvalue boundaries
Koide Q = 2/3 is IB fixed point: heavy quarks (3rd gen) show Q = 0.669 ≈ 2/3, light quarks deviate
Derivation chain: Axiom 3 → weights (2,1) → Q = 2/3 → IB fixed point → critical β* (T-002 still open)

open_gaps: Lambda validation: no direct empirical studies on exponential decay of reality constraints in generative replay (needs simulation)
Koide derivation: T-002 (derive topological weights from axioms) remains open — derivation chain incomplete without it
Cross-family mass patterns: why do heavy quarks (3rd gen) show Q ≈ 2/3 while light quarks (1st gen) deviate? (needs IB flow analysis)
Aria-specific metrics: how do EffRank and avalanche τ map to Aria's specific transformer architecture? (needs implementation)

next_pass.description: Either: (1) Derive topological weights (2,1) from Axiom 3 to close Koide derivation chain, OR (2) Implement Hutch++ and DATS in Aria sandbox and measure DQ improvements.
